net.ACT_7_7: But I will punish the nation they serve as slaves,’ said God, ‘and after these things they will come out of there and worship me in this place.’
net.ACT_7_8: Then God gave Abraham the covenant of circumcision, and so he became the father of Isaac and circumcised him when he was eight days old, and Isaac became the father of Jacob, and Jacob of the twelve patriarchs.
net.ACT_7_9: The patriarchs, because they were jealous of Joseph, sold him into Egypt. But God was with him,
net.ACT_7_10: and rescued him from all his troubles, and granted him favor and wisdom in the presence of Pharaoh, king of Egypt, who made him ruler over Egypt and over all his household.
net.ACT_7_11: Then a famine occurred throughout Egypt and Canaan, causing great suffering, and our ancestors could not find food.
net.ACT_7_12: So when Jacob heard that there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there the first time.
net.ACT_7_13: On their second visit Joseph made himself known to his brothers again, and Joseph’s family became known to Pharaoh.
net.ACT_7_14: So Joseph sent a message and invited his father Jacob and all his relatives to come, seventy-five people in all.
net.ACT_7_15: So Jacob went down to Egypt and died there, along with our ancestors,
net.ACT_7_16: and their bones were later moved to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had bought for a certain sum of money from the sons of Hamor in Shechem.
net.ACT_7_17: “But as the time drew near for God to fulfill the promise he had declared to Abraham, the people increased greatly in number in Egypt,
net.ACT_7_18: until another king who did not know about Joseph ruled over Egypt.
net.ACT_7_19: This was the one who exploited our people and was cruel to our ancestors, forcing them to abandon their infants so they would die.
net.ACT_7_20: At that time Moses was born, and he was beautiful to God. For three months he was brought up in his father’s house,
net.ACT_7_21: and when he had been abandoned, Pharaoh’s daughter adopted him and brought him up as her own son.
net.ACT_7_22: So Moses was trained in all the wisdom of the Egyptians and was powerful in his words and deeds.
net.ACT_7_23: But when he was about forty years old, it entered his mind to visit his fellow countrymen the Israelites.
net.ACT_7_24: When he saw one of them being hurt unfairly, Moses came to his defense and avenged the person who was mistreated by striking down the Egyptian.
net.ACT_7_25: He thought his own people would understand that God was delivering them through him, but they did not understand.
net.ACT_7_26: The next day Moses saw two men fighting, and tried to make peace between them, saying, ‘Men, you are brothers; why are you hurting one another?’
net.ACT_7_27: But the man who was unfairly hurting his neighbor pushed Moses aside, saying, ‘Who made you a ruler and judge over us?
net.ACT_7_28: You don’t want to kill me the way you killed the Egyptian yesterday, do you?’
net.ACT_7_29: When the man said this, Moses fled and became a foreigner in the land of Midian, where he became the father of two sons.
net.ACT_7_30: “After forty years had passed, an angel appeared to him in the desert of Mount Sinai, in the flame of a burning bush.
net.ACT_7_31: When Moses saw it, he was amazed at the sight, and when he approached to investigate, there came the voice of the Lord,
net.ACT_7_32: ‘I am the God of your forefathers, the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.’ Moses began to tremble and did not dare to look more closely.
net.ACT_7_33: But the Lord said to him, ‘Take the sandals off your feet, for the place where you are standing is holy ground.
net.ACT_7_34: I have certainly seen the suffering of my people who are in Egypt and have heard their groaning, and I have come down to rescue them. Now come, I will send you to Egypt.’
net.ACT_7_35: This same Moses they had rejected, saying, ‘Who made you a ruler and judge?’ God sent as both ruler and deliverer through the hand of the angel who appeared to him in the bush.
net.ACT_7_36: This man led them out, performing wonders and miraculous signs in the land of Egypt, at the Red Sea, and in the wilderness for forty years.
net.ACT_7_37: This is the Moses who said to the Israelites, ‘God will raise up for you a prophet like me from among your brothers.’
net.ACT_7_38: This is the man who was in the congregation in the wilderness with the angel who spoke to him at Mount Sinai, and with our ancestors, and he received living oracles to give to you.
net.ACT_7_39: Our ancestors were unwilling to obey him, but pushed him aside and turned back to Egypt in their hearts,
net.ACT_7_40: saying to Aaron, ‘Make us gods who will go in front of us, for this Moses, who led us out of the land of Egypt —we do not know what has happened to him!’
net.ACT_7_41: At that time they made an idol in the form of a calf, brought a sacrifice to the idol, and began rejoicing in the works of their hands.
net.ACT_7_42: But God turned away from them and gave them over to worship the host of heaven, as it is written in the book of the prophets: ‘It was not to me that you offered slain animals and sacrifices forty years in the wilderness, was it, house of Israel?
net.ACT_7_43: But you took along the tabernacle of Moloch and the star of the god Rephan, the images you made to worship, but I will deport you beyond Babylon.’
net.ACT_7_44: Our ancestors had the tabernacle of testimony in the wilderness, just as God who spoke to Moses ordered him to make it according to the design he had seen.
net.ACT_7_45: Our ancestors received possession of it and brought it in with Joshua when they dispossessed the nations that God drove out before our ancestors, until the time of David.
net.ACT_7_46: He found favor with God and asked that he could find a dwelling place for the house of Jacob.
net.ACT_7_47: But Solomon built a house for him.
net.ACT_7_48: Yet the Most High does not live in houses made by human hands, as the prophet says,
net.ACT_7_49: ‘Heaven is my throne, and earth is the footstool for my feet. What kind of house will you build for me, says the Lord, or what is my resting place?
net.ACT_7_50: Did my hand not make all these things?’
net.ACT_7_51: “You stubborn people, with uncircumcised hearts and ears! You are always resisting the Holy Spirit, like your ancestors did!
net.ACT_7_52: Which of the prophets did your ancestors not persecute? They killed those who foretold long ago the coming of the Righteous One, whose betrayers and murderers you have now become!
net.ACT_7_53: You received the law by decrees given by angels, but you did not obey it.”
net.ACT_7_54: When they heard these things, they became furious and ground their teeth at him.
net.ACT_7_55: But Stephen, full of the Holy Spirit, looked intently toward heaven and saw the glory of God, and Jesus standing at the right hand of God.
net.ACT_7_56: “Look!” he said. “I see the heavens opened, and the Son of Man standing at the right hand of God!”
net.ACT_7_57: But they covered their ears, shouting out with a loud voice, and rushed at him with one intent.
net.ACT_7_58: When they had driven him out of the city, they began to stone him, and the witnesses laid their cloaks at the feet of a young man named Saul.
net.ACT_7_59: They continued to stone Stephen while he prayed, “Lord Jesus, receive my spirit!”
net.ACT_7_60: Then he fell to his knees and cried out with a loud voice, “Lord, do not hold this sin against them!” When he had said this, he died.
net.ACT_8_1: And Saul agreed completely with killing him. Saul Begins to Persecute the Church Now on that day a great persecution began against the church in Jerusalem, and all except the apostles were forced to scatter throughout the regions of Judea and Samaria.
net.ACT_8_2: Some devout men buried Stephen and made loud lamentation over him.
net.ACT_8_3: But Saul was trying to destroy the church; entering one house after another, he dragged off both men and women and put them in prison. Philip Preaches in Samaria
net.ACT_8_4: Now those who had been forced to scatter went around proclaiming the good news of the word.
net.ACT_8_5: Philip went down to the main city of Samaria and began proclaiming the Christ to them.
net.ACT_8_6: The crowds were paying attention with one mind to what Philip said, as they heard and saw the miraculous signs he was performing.
net.ACT_8_7: For unclean spirits, crying with loud shrieks, were coming out of many who were possessed, and many paralyzed and lame people were healed.
net.ACT_8_8: So there was great joy in that city.
net.ACT_8_9: Now in that city was a man named Simon, who had been practicing magic and amazing the people of Samaria, claiming to be someone great.
net.ACT_8_10: All the people, from the least to the greatest, paid close attention to him, saying, “This man is the power of God that is called ‘Great.’”
net.ACT_8_11: And they paid close attention to him because he had amazed them for a long time with his magic.
net.ACT_8_12: But when they believed Philip as he was proclaiming the good news about the kingdom of God and the name of Jesus Christ, they began to be baptized, both men and women.
net.ACT_8_13: Even Simon himself believed, and after he was baptized, he stayed close to Philip constantly, and when he saw the signs and great miracles that were occurring, he was amazed.
net.ACT_8_14: Now when the apostles in Jerusalem heard that Samaria had accepted the word of God, they sent Peter and John to them.
net.ACT_8_15: These two went down and prayed for them so that they would receive the Holy Spirit.
net.ACT_8_16: (For the Spirit had not yet come upon any of them, but they had only been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.)
net.ACT_8_17: Then Peter and John placed their hands on the Samaritans, and they received the Holy Spirit.
net.ACT_8_18: Now Simon, when he saw that the Spirit was given through the laying on of the apostles’ hands, offered them money,
net.ACT_8_19: saying, “Give me this power too, so that everyone I place my hands on may receive the Holy Spirit.”
net.ACT_8_20: But Peter said to him, “May your silver perish with you, because you thought you could acquire God’s gift with money!
net.ACT_8_21: You have no share or part in this matter because your heart is not right before God!
net.ACT_8_22: Therefore repent of this wickedness of yours, and pray to the Lord that he may perhaps forgive you for the intent of your heart.
net.ACT_8_23: For I see that you are bitterly envious and in bondage to sin.”
net.ACT_8_24: But Simon replied, “You pray to the Lord for me so that nothing of what you have said may happen to me.”
net.ACT_8_25: So after Peter and John had solemnly testified and spoken the word of the Lord, they started back to Jerusalem, proclaiming the good news to many Samaritan villages as they went. Philip and the Ethiopian Eunuch
net.ACT_8_26: Then an angel of the Lord said to Philip, “Get up and go south on the road that goes down from Jerusalem to Gaza.” (This is a desert road.)
net.ACT_8_27: So he got up and went. There he met an Ethiopian eunuch, a court official of Candace, queen of the Ethiopians, who was in charge of all her treasury. He had come to Jerusalem to worship,
net.ACT_8_28: and was returning home, sitting in his chariot, reading the prophet Isaiah.
net.ACT_8_29: Then the Spirit said to Philip, “Go over and join this chariot.”
net.ACT_8_30: So Philip ran up to it and heard the man reading the prophet Isaiah. He asked him, “Do you understand what you’re reading?”
net.ACT_8_31: The man replied, “How in the world can I, unless someone guides me?” So he invited Philip to come up and sit with him.
net.ACT_8_32: Now the passage of scripture the man was reading was this: “He was led like a sheep to slaughter, and like a lamb before its shearer is silent, so he did not open his mouth.
net.ACT_8_33: In humiliation justice was taken from him. Who can describe his posterity? For his life was taken away from the earth.”
net.ACT_8_34: Then the eunuch said to Philip, “Please tell me, who is the prophet saying this about—himself or someone else?”
net.ACT_8_35: So Philip started speaking, and beginning with this scripture proclaimed the good news about Jesus to him.
net.ACT_8_36: Now as they were going along the road, they came to some water, and the eunuch said, “Look, there is water! What is to stop me from being baptized?”
net.ACT_8_38: So he ordered the chariot to stop, and both Philip and the eunuch went down into the water, and Philip baptized him.
net.ACT_8_39: Now when they came up out of the water, the Spirit of the Lord snatched Philip away, and the eunuch did not see him any more, but went on his way rejoicing.
net.ACT_8_40: Philip, however, found himself at Azotus, and as he passed through the area, he proclaimed the good news to all the towns until he came to Caesarea.
net.ACT_9_1: Meanwhile Saul, still breathing out threats to murder the Lord’s disciples, went to the high priest
net.ACT_9_2: and requested letters from him to the synagogues in Damascus, so that if he found any who belonged to the Way, either men or women, he could bring them as prisoners to Jerusalem.
net.ACT_9_3: As he was going along, approaching Damascus, suddenly a light from heaven flashed around him.
net.ACT_9_4: He fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to him, “Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?”
net.ACT_9_5: So he said, “Who are you, Lord?” He replied, “I am Jesus whom you are persecuting!
net.ACT_9_6: But stand up and enter the city and you will be told what you must do.”
net.ACT_9_7: (Now the men who were traveling with him stood there speechless, because they heard the voice but saw no one.)
net.ACT_9_8: So Saul got up from the ground, but although his eyes were open, he could see nothing. Leading him by the hand, his companions brought him into Damascus.
net.ACT_9_9: For three days he could not see, and he neither ate nor drank anything.
net.ACT_9_10: Now there was a disciple in Damascus named Ananias. The Lord said to him in a vision, “Ananias,” and he replied, “Here I am, Lord.”
net.ACT_9_11: Then the Lord told him, “Get up and go to the street called ‘Straight,’ and at Judas’ house look for a man from Tarsus named Saul. For he is praying,
net.ACT_9_12: and he has seen in a vision a man named Ananias come in and place his hands on him so that he may see again.”
net.ACT_9_13: But Ananias replied, “Lord, I have heard from many people about this man, how much harm he has done to your saints in Jerusalem,
net.ACT_9_14: and here he has authority from the chief priests to imprison all who call on your name!”
net.ACT_9_15: But the Lord said to him, “Go, because this man is my chosen instrument to carry my name before Gentiles and kings and the people of Israel.
net.ACT_9_16: For I will show him how much he must suffer for the sake of my name.”
net.ACT_9_17: So Ananias departed and entered the house, placed his hands on Saul and said, “Brother Saul, the Lord Jesus, who appeared to you on the road as you came here, has sent me so that you may see again and be filled with the Holy Spirit.”
net.ACT_9_18: Immediately something like scales fell from his eyes, and he could see again. He got up and was baptized,
net.ACT_9_19: and after taking some food, his strength returned. For several days he was with the disciples in Damascus,
net.ACT_9_20: and immediately he began to proclaim Jesus in the synagogues, saying, “This man is the Son of God.”
net.ACT_9_21: All who heard him were amazed and were saying, “Is this not the man who in Jerusalem was ravaging those who call on this name, and who had come here to bring them as prisoners to the chief priests?”
net.ACT_9_22: But Saul became more and more capable, and was causing consternation among the Jews who lived in Damascus by proving that Jesus is the Christ. Saul’s Escape from Damascus
net.ACT_9_23: Now after some days had passed, the Jews plotted together to kill him,
net.ACT_9_24: but Saul learned of their plot against him. They were also watching the city gates day and night so that they could kill him.
net.ACT_9_25: But his disciples took him at night and let him down through an opening in the wall by lowering him in a basket. Saul Returns to Jerusalem
net.ACT_9_26: When he arrived in Jerusalem, he attempted to associate with the disciples, and they were all afraid of him, because they did not believe that he was a disciple.
net.ACT_9_27: But Barnabas took Saul, brought him to the apostles, and related to them how he had seen the Lord on the road, that the Lord had spoken to him, and how in Damascus he had spoken out boldly in the name of Jesus.
net.ACT_9_28: So he was staying with them, associating openly with them in Jerusalem, speaking out boldly in the name of the Lord.
net.ACT_9_29: He was speaking and debating with the Greek-speaking Jews, but they were trying to kill him.
net.ACT_9_30: When the brothers found out about this, they brought him down to Caesarea and sent him away to Tarsus.
net.ACT_9_31: Then the church throughout Judea, Galilee, and Samaria experienced peace and thus was strengthened. Living in the fear of the Lord and in the encouragement of the Holy Spirit, the church increased in numbers. Peter Heals Aeneas
net.ACT_9_32: Now as Peter was traveling around from place to place, he also came down to the saints who lived in Lydda.
net.ACT_9_33: He found there a man named Aeneas who had been confined to a mattress for eight years because he was paralyzed.
net.ACT_9_34: Peter said to him, “Aeneas, Jesus the Christ heals you. Get up and make your own bed!” And immediately he got up.
net.ACT_9_35: All those who lived in Lydda and Sharon saw him, and they turned to the Lord. Peter Raises Dorcas
net.ACT_9_36: Now in Joppa there was a disciple named Tabitha (which in translation means Dorcas). She was continually doing good deeds and acts of charity.
net.ACT_9_37: At that time she became sick and died. When they had washed her body, they placed it in an upstairs room.
net.ACT_9_38: Because Lydda was near Joppa, when the disciples heard that Peter was there, they sent two men to him and urged him, “Come to us without delay.”
net.ACT_9_39: So Peter got up and went with them, and when he arrived they brought him to the upper room. All the widows stood beside him, crying and showing him the tunics and other clothing Dorcas used to make while she was with them.
net.ACT_9_40: But Peter sent them all outside, knelt down, and prayed. Turning to the body, he said, “Tabitha, get up.” Then she opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter, she sat up.
net.ACT_9_41: He gave her his hand and helped her get up. Then he called the saints and widows and presented her alive.
net.ACT_9_42: This became known throughout all Joppa, and many believed in the Lord.
net.ACT_9_43: So Peter stayed many days in Joppa with a man named Simon, a tanner.
net.ACT_10_1: Now there was a man in Caesarea named Cornelius, a centurion of what was known as the Italian Cohort.
net.ACT_10_2: He was a devout, God-fearing man, as was all his household; he did many acts of charity for the people and prayed to God regularly.
net.ACT_10_3: About three o’clock one afternoon he saw clearly in a vision an angel of God who came in and said to him, “Cornelius.”
net.ACT_10_4: Staring at him and becoming greatly afraid, Cornelius replied, “What is it, Lord?” The angel said to him, “Your prayers and your acts of charity have gone up as a memorial before God.
net.ACT_10_5: Now send men to Joppa and summon a man named Simon, who is called Peter.
net.ACT_10_6: This man is staying as a guest with a man named Simon, a tanner, whose house is by the sea.”
net.ACT_10_7: When the angel who had spoken to him departed, Cornelius called two of his personal servants and a devout soldier from among those who served him,
net.ACT_10_8: and when he had explained everything to them, he sent them to Joppa.
net.ACT_10_9: About noon the next day, while they were on their way and approaching the city, Peter went up on the roof to pray.
net.ACT_10_10: He became hungry and wanted to eat, but while they were preparing the meal, a trance came over him.
net.ACT_10_11: He saw heaven opened and an object something like a large sheet descending, being let down to earth by its four corners.
net.ACT_10_12: In it were all kinds of four-footed animals and reptiles of the earth and wild birds.
net.ACT_10_13: Then a voice said to him, “Get up, Peter; slaughter and eat!”
net.ACT_10_14: But Peter said, “Certainly not, Lord, for I have never eaten anything defiled and ritually unclean!”
net.ACT_10_15: The voice spoke to him again, a second time, “What God has made clean, you must not consider ritually unclean!”
net.ACT_10_16: This happened three times, and immediately the object was taken up into heaven.
net.ACT_10_17: Now while Peter was puzzling over what the vision he had seen could signify, the men sent by Cornelius had learned where Simon’s house was and approached the gate.
net.ACT_10_18: They called out to ask if Simon, known as Peter, was staying there as a guest.
net.ACT_10_19: While Peter was still thinking seriously about the vision, the Spirit said to him, “Look! Three men are looking for you.
net.ACT_10_20: But get up, go down, and accompany them without hesitation, because I have sent them.”
net.ACT_10_21: So Peter went down to the men and said, “Here I am, the person you’re looking for. Why have you come?”
net.ACT_10_22: They said, “Cornelius the centurion, a righteous and God-fearing man, well spoken of by the whole Jewish nation, was directed by a holy angel to summon you to his house and to hear a message from you.”
net.ACT_10_23: So Peter invited them in and entertained them as guests. On the next day he got up and set out with them, and some of the brothers from Joppa accompanied him.
net.ACT_10_24: The following day he entered Caesarea. Now Cornelius was waiting anxiously for them and had called together his relatives and close friends.
net.ACT_10_25: So when Peter came in, Cornelius met him, fell at his feet, and worshiped him.
net.ACT_10_26: But Peter helped him up, saying, “Stand up. I too am a mere mortal.”
net.ACT_10_27: Peter continued talking with him as he went in, and he found many people gathered together.
net.ACT_10_28: He said to them, “You know that it is unlawful for a Jew to associate with or visit a Gentile, yet God has shown me that I should call no person defiled or ritually unclean.
net.ACT_10_29: Therefore when you sent for me, I came without any objection. Now may I ask why you sent for me?”
net.ACT_10_30: Cornelius replied, “Four days ago at this very hour, at three o’clock in the afternoon, I was praying in my house, and suddenly a man in shining clothing stood before me
net.ACT_10_31: and said, ‘Cornelius, your prayer has been heard and your acts of charity have been remembered before God.
net.ACT_10_32: Therefore send to Joppa and summon Simon, who is called Peter. This man is staying as a guest in the house of Simon the tanner, by the sea.’
net.ACT_10_33: Therefore I sent for you at once, and you were kind enough to come. So now we are all here in the presence of God to listen to everything the Lord has commanded you to say to us.”
net.ACT_10_34: Then Peter started speaking: “I now truly understand that God does not show favoritism in dealing with people,
net.ACT_10_35: but in every nation the person who fears him and does what is right is welcomed before him.
net.ACT_10_36: You know the message he sent to the people of Israel, proclaiming the good news of peace through Jesus Christ (he is Lord of all)—
net.ACT_10_37: you know what happened throughout Judea, beginning from Galilee after the baptism that John announced:
net.ACT_10_38: with respect to Jesus from Nazareth, that God anointed him with the Holy Spirit and with power. He went around doing good and healing all who were oppressed by the devil, because God was with him.
net.ACT_10_39: We are witnesses of all the things he did both in Judea and in Jerusalem. They killed him by hanging him on a tree,
net.ACT_10_40: but God raised him up on the third day and caused him to be seen,
net.ACT_10_41: not by all the people, but by us, the witnesses God had already chosen, who ate and drank with him after he rose from the dead.
net.ACT_10_42: He commanded us to preach to the people and to warn them that he is the one appointed by God as judge of the living and the dead.
net.ACT_10_43: About him all the prophets testify, that everyone who believes in him receives forgiveness of sins through his name.” The Gentiles Receive the Holy Spirit
net.ACT_10_44: While Peter was still speaking these words, the Holy Spirit fell on all those who heard the message.
net.ACT_10_45: The circumcised believers who had accompanied Peter were greatly astonished that the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out even on the Gentiles,
net.ACT_10_46: for they heard them speaking in tongues and praising God. Then Peter said,
net.ACT_10_47: “No one can withhold the water for these people to be baptized, who have received the Holy Spirit just as we did, can he?”
net.ACT_10_48: So he gave orders to have them baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they asked him to stay for several days.
net.ACT_11_1: Now the apostles and the brothers who were throughout Judea heard that the Gentiles too had accepted the word of God.
net.ACT_11_2: So when Peter went up to Jerusalem, the circumcised believers took issue with him,
net.ACT_11_3: saying, “You went to uncircumcised men and shared a meal with them.”
net.ACT_11_4: But Peter began and explained it to them point by point, saying,
net.ACT_11_5: “I was in the city of Joppa praying, and in a trance I saw a vision, an object something like a large sheet descending, being let down from heaven by its four corners, and it came to me.
net.ACT_11_6: As I stared I looked into it and saw four-footed animals of the earth, wild animals, reptiles, and wild birds.
net.ACT_11_7: I also heard a voice saying to me, ‘Get up, Peter; slaughter and eat!’
net.ACT_11_8: But I said, ‘Certainly not, Lord, for nothing defiled or ritually unclean has ever entered my mouth!’
net.ACT_11_9: But the voice replied a second time from heaven, ‘What God has made clean, you must not consider ritually unclean!’
net.ACT_11_10: This happened three times, and then everything was pulled up to heaven again.
net.ACT_11_11: At that very moment, three men sent to me from Caesarea approached the house where we were staying.
net.ACT_11_12: The Spirit told me to accompany them without hesitation. These six brothers also went with me, and we entered the man’s house.
net.ACT_11_13: He informed us how he had seen an angel standing in his house and saying, ‘Send to Joppa and summon Simon, who is called Peter,
net.ACT_11_14: who will speak a message to you by which you and your entire household will be saved.’
net.ACT_11_15: Then as I began to speak, the Holy Spirit fell on them just as he did on us at the beginning.
net.ACT_11_16: And I remembered the word of the Lord, as he used to say, ‘John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.’
net.ACT_11_17: Therefore if God gave them the same gift as he also gave us after believing in the Lord Jesus Christ, who was I to hinder God?”
net.ACT_11_18: When they heard this, they ceased their objections and praised God, saying, “So then, God has granted the repentance that leads to life even to the Gentiles.” Activity in the Church at Antioch
net.ACT_11_19: Now those who had been scattered because of the persecution that took place over Stephen went as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, speaking the message to no one but Jews.
net.ACT_11_20: But there were some men from Cyprus and Cyrene among them who came to Antioch and began to speak to the Greeks too, proclaiming the good news of the Lord Jesus.
net.ACT_11_21: The hand of the Lord was with them, and a great number who believed turned to the Lord.
net.ACT_11_22: A report about them came to the attention of the church in Jerusalem, and they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
net.ACT_11_23: When he came and saw the grace of God, he rejoiced and encouraged them all to remain true to the Lord with devoted hearts,
net.ACT_11_24: because he was a good man, full of the Holy Spirit and of faith, and a significant number of people were brought to the Lord.
net.ACT_11_25: Then Barnabas departed for Tarsus to look for Saul,
net.ACT_11_26: and when he found him, he brought him to Antioch. So for a whole year Barnabas and Saul met with the church and taught a significant number of people. Now it was in Antioch that the disciples were first called Christians. Famine Relief for Judea
net.ACT_11_27: At that time some prophets came down from Jerusalem to Antioch.
net.ACT_11_28: One of them, named Agabus, got up and predicted by the Spirit that a severe famine was about to come over the whole inhabited world. (This took place during the reign of Claudius.)
net.ACT_11_29: So the disciples, each in accordance with his financial ability, decided to send relief to the brothers living in Judea.
net.ACT_11_30: They did so, sending their financial aid to the elders by Barnabas and Saul.
net.ACT_12_1: About that time King Herod laid hands on some from the church to harm them.
net.ACT_12_2: He had James, the brother of John, executed with a sword.
net.ACT_12_3: When he saw that this pleased the Jews, he proceeded to arrest Peter too. (This took place during the feast of Unleavened Bread.)
net.ACT_12_4: When he had seized him, he put him in prison, handing him over to four squads of soldiers to guard him. Herod planned to bring him out for public trial after the Passover.
net.ACT_12_5: So Peter was kept in prison, but those in the church were earnestly praying to God for him.
net.ACT_12_6: On that very night before Herod was going to bring him out for trial, Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains, while guards in front of the door were keeping watch over the prison.
net.ACT_12_7: Suddenly an angel of the Lord appeared, and a light shone in the prison cell. He struck Peter on the side and woke him up, saying, “Get up quickly!” And the chains fell off Peter’s wrists.
net.ACT_12_8: The angel said to him, “Fasten your belt and put on your sandals.” Peter did so. Then the angel said to him, “Put on your cloak and follow me.”
net.ACT_12_9: Peter went out and followed him; he did not realize that what was happening through the angel was real, but thought he was seeing a vision.
net.ACT_12_10: After they had passed the first and second guards, they came to the iron gate leading into the city. It opened for them by itself, and they went outside and walked down one narrow street, when at once the angel left him.
net.ACT_12_11: When Peter came to himself, he said, “Now I know for certain that the Lord has sent his angel and rescued me from the hand of Herod and from everything the Jewish people were expecting to happen.”
net.ACT_12_12: When Peter realized this, he went to the house of Mary, the mother of John Mark, where many people had gathered together and were praying.
net.ACT_12_13: When he knocked at the door of the outer gate, a slave girl named Rhoda answered.
net.ACT_12_14: When she recognized Peter’s voice, she was so overjoyed she did not open the gate, but ran back in and told them that Peter was standing at the gate.
net.ACT_12_15: But they said to her, “You’ve lost your mind!” But she kept insisting that it was Peter, and they kept saying, “It is his angel!”
net.ACT_12_16: Now Peter continued knocking, and when they opened the door and saw him, they were greatly astonished.
net.ACT_12_17: He motioned to them with his hand to be quiet and then related how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. He said, “Tell James and the brothers these things,” and then he left and went to another place.
net.ACT_12_18: At daybreak there was great consternation among the soldiers over what had become of Peter.
net.ACT_12_19: When Herod had searched for him and did not find him, he questioned the guards and commanded that they be led away to execution. Then Herod went down from Judea to Caesarea and stayed there.
net.ACT_12_20: Now Herod was having an angry quarrel with the people of Tyre and Sidon. So they joined together and presented themselves before him. And after convincing Blastus, the king’s personal assistant, to help them, they asked for peace, because their country’s food supply was provided by the king’s country.
net.ACT_12_21: On a day determined in advance, Herod put on his royal robes, sat down on the judgment seat, and made a speech to them.
net.ACT_12_22: But the crowd began to shout, “The voice of a god, and not of a man!”
net.ACT_12_23: Immediately an angel of the Lord struck Herod down because he did not give the glory to God, and he was eaten by worms and died.
net.ACT_12_24: But the word of God kept on increasing and multiplying.
net.ACT_12_25: So Barnabas and Saul returned to Jerusalem when they had completed their mission, bringing along with them John Mark.
net.ACT_13_1: Now there were these prophets and teachers in the church at Antioch: Barnabas, Simeon called Niger, Lucius the Cyrenian, Manaen (a close friend of Herod the tetrarch from childhood) and Saul.
net.ACT_13_2: While they were serving the Lord and fasting, the Holy Spirit said, “Set apart for me Barnabas and Saul for the work to which I have called them.”
net.ACT_13_3: Then, after they had fasted and prayed and placed their hands on them, they sent them off. Paul and Barnabas Preach in Cyprus
net.ACT_13_4: So Barnabas and Saul, sent out by the Holy Spirit, went down to Seleucia, and from there they sailed to Cyprus.
net.ACT_13_5: When they arrived in Salamis, they began to proclaim the word of God in the Jewish synagogues. (Now they also had John as their assistant.)
net.ACT_13_6: When they had crossed over the whole island as far as Paphos, they found a magician, a Jewish false prophet named Bar-Jesus,
net.ACT_13_7: who was with the proconsul Sergius Paulus, an intelligent man. The proconsul summoned Barnabas and Saul and wanted to hear the word of God.
net.ACT_13_8: But the magician Elymas (for that is the way his name is translated) opposed them, trying to turn the proconsul away from the faith.
net.ACT_13_9: But Saul (also known as Paul), filled with the Holy Spirit, stared straight at him
net.ACT_13_10: and said, “You who are full of all deceit and all wrongdoing, you son of the devil, you enemy of all righteousness—will you not stop making crooked the straight paths of the Lord?
net.ACT_13_11: Now look, the hand of the Lord is against you, and you will be blind, unable to see the sun for a time!” Immediately mistiness and darkness came over him, and he went around seeking people to lead him by the hand.
net.ACT_13_12: Then when the proconsul saw what had happened, he believed, because he was greatly astounded at the teaching about the Lord. Paul and Barnabas at Pisidian Antioch
net.ACT_13_13: Then Paul and his companions put out to sea from Paphos and came to Perga in Pamphylia, but John left them and returned to Jerusalem.
net.ACT_13_14: Moving on from Perga, they arrived at Pisidian Antioch, and on the Sabbath day they went into the synagogue and sat down.
net.ACT_13_15: After the reading from the law and the prophets, the leaders of the synagogue sent them a message, saying, “Brothers, if you have any message of exhortation for the people, speak it.”
net.ACT_13_16: So Paul stood up, gestured with his hand and said, “Men of Israel, and you Gentiles who fear God, listen:
net.ACT_13_17: The God of this people Israel chose our ancestors and made the people great during their stay as foreigners in the country of Egypt, and with uplifted arm he led them out of it.
net.ACT_13_18: For a period of about forty years he put up with them in the wilderness.
net.ACT_13_19: After he had destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan, he gave his people their land as an inheritance.
net.ACT_13_20: All this took about 450 years. After this he gave them judges until the time of Samuel the prophet.
net.ACT_13_21: Then they asked for a king, and God gave them Saul son of Kish, a man from the tribe of Benjamin, who ruled forty years.
net.ACT_13_22: After removing him, God raised up David their king. He testified about him: ‘I have found David the son of Jesse to be a man after my heart, who will accomplish everything I want him to do.’
net.ACT_13_23: From the descendants of this man God brought to Israel a Savior, Jesus, just as he promised.
net.ACT_13_24: Before Jesus arrived, John had proclaimed a baptism for repentance to all the people of Israel.
net.ACT_13_25: But while John was completing his mission, he said repeatedly, ‘What do you think I am? I am not he. But look, one is coming after me. I am not worthy to untie the sandals on his feet!’
net.ACT_13_26: Brothers, descendants of Abraham’s family, and those Gentiles among you who fear God, the message of this salvation has been sent to us.
net.ACT_13_27: For the people who live in Jerusalem and their rulers did not recognize him, and they fulfilled the sayings of the prophets that are read every Sabbath by condemning him.
net.ACT_13_28: Though they found no basis for a death sentence, they asked Pilate to have him executed.
net.ACT_13_29: When they had accomplished everything that was written about him, they took him down from the cross and placed him in a tomb.
net.ACT_13_30: But God raised him from the dead,
net.ACT_13_31: and for many days he appeared to those who had accompanied him from Galilee to Jerusalem. These are now his witnesses to the people.
net.ACT_13_32: And we proclaim to you the good news about the promise to our ancestors,
net.ACT_13_33: that this promise God has fulfilled to us, their children, by raising Jesus, as also it is written in the second psalm, ‘You are my Son; today I have fathered you.’
net.ACT_13_34: But regarding the fact that he has raised Jesus from the dead, never again to be in a state of decay, God has spoken in this way: ‘I will give you the holy and trustworthy promises made to David.’
net.ACT_13_35: Therefore he also says in another psalm, ‘You will not permit your Holy One to experience decay.’
net.ACT_13_36: For David, after he had served God’s purpose in his own generation, died, was buried with his ancestors, and experienced decay,
net.ACT_13_37: but the one whom God raised up did not experience decay.
net.ACT_13_38: Therefore let it be known to you, brothers, that through this one forgiveness of sins is proclaimed to you,
net.ACT_13_39: and by this one everyone who believes is justified from everything from which the law of Moses could not justify you.
net.ACT_13_40: Watch out, then, that what is spoken about by the prophets does not happen to you:
net.ACT_13_41: ‘Look, you scoffers; be amazed and perish! For I am doing a work in your days, a work you would never believe, even if someone tells you.’”
net.ACT_13_42: As Paul and Barnabas were going out, the people were urging them to speak about these things on the next Sabbath.
net.ACT_13_43: When the meeting of the synagogue had broken up, many of the Jews and God-fearing proselytes followed Paul and Barnabas, who were speaking with them and were persuading them to continue in the grace of God.
net.ACT_13_44: On the next Sabbath almost the whole city assembled together to hear the word of the Lord.
net.ACT_13_45: But when the Jews saw the crowds, they were filled with jealousy, and they began to contradict what Paul was saying by reviling him.
net.ACT_13_46: Both Paul and Barnabas replied courageously, “It was necessary to speak the word of God to you first. Since you reject it and do not consider yourselves worthy of eternal life, we are turning to the Gentiles.
net.ACT_13_47: For this is what the Lord has commanded us: ‘I have appointed you to be a light for the Gentiles, to bring salvation to the ends of the earth.’”
net.ACT_13_48: When the Gentiles heard this, they began to rejoice and praise the word of the Lord, and all who had been appointed for eternal life believed.
net.ACT_13_49: So the word of the Lord was spreading through the entire region.
net.ACT_13_50: But the Jews incited the God-fearing women of high social standing and the prominent men of the city, stirred up persecution against Paul and Barnabas, and threw them out of their region.
net.ACT_13_51: So after they shook the dust off their feet in protest against them, they went to Iconium.
net.ACT_13_52: And the disciples were filled with joy and with the Holy Spirit.
net.ACT_14_1: The same thing happened in Iconium when Paul and Barnabas went into the Jewish synagogue and spoke in such a way that a large group of both Jews and Greeks believed.
net.ACT_14_2: But the Jews who refused to believe stirred up the Gentiles and poisoned their minds against the brothers.
net.ACT_14_3: So they stayed there for a considerable time, speaking out courageously for the Lord, who testified to the message of his grace, granting miraculous signs and wonders to be performed through their hands.
net.ACT_14_4: But the population of the city was divided; some sided with the Jews, and some with the apostles.
net.ACT_14_5: When both the Gentiles and the Jews (together with their rulers) made an attempt to mistreat them and stone them,
net.ACT_14_6: Paul and Barnabas learned about it and fled to the Lycaonian cities of Lystra and Derbe and the surrounding region.
net.ACT_14_7: There they continued to proclaim the good news. Paul and Barnabas at Lystra
net.ACT_14_8: In Lystra sat a man who could not use his feet, lame from birth, who had never walked.
net.ACT_14_9: This man was listening to Paul as he was speaking. When Paul stared intently at him and saw he had faith to be healed,
net.ACT_14_10: he said with a loud voice, “Stand upright on your feet.” And the man leaped up and began walking.
net.ACT_14_11: So when the crowds saw what Paul had done, they shouted in the Lycaonian language, “The gods have come down to us in human form!”
net.ACT_14_12: They began to call Barnabas Zeus and Paul Hermes, because he was the chief speaker.
net.ACT_14_13: The priest of the temple of Zeus, located just outside the city, brought bulls and garlands to the city gates; he and the crowds wanted to offer sacrifices to them.
net.ACT_14_14: But when the apostles Barnabas and Paul heard about it, they tore their clothes and rushed out into the crowd, shouting,
net.ACT_14_15: “Men, why are you doing these things? We too are men, with human natures just like you! We are proclaiming the good news to you, so that you should turn from these worthless things to the living God, who made the heaven, the earth, the sea, and everything that is in them.
net.ACT_14_16: In past generations he allowed all the nations to go their own ways,
net.ACT_14_17: yet he did not leave himself without a witness by doing good, by giving you rain from heaven and fruitful seasons, satisfying you with food and your hearts with joy.”
net.ACT_14_18: Even by saying these things, they scarcely persuaded the crowds not to offer sacrifice to them.
net.ACT_14_19: But Jews came from Antioch and Iconium, and after winning the crowds over, they stoned Paul and dragged him out of the city, presuming him to be dead.
net.ACT_14_20: But after the disciples had surrounded him, he got up and went back into the city. On the next day he left with Barnabas for Derbe. Paul and Barnabas Return to Antioch in Syria
net.ACT_14_21: After they had proclaimed the good news in that city and made many disciples, they returned to Lystra, to Iconium, and to Antioch.
net.ACT_14_22: They strengthened the souls of the disciples and encouraged them to continue in the faith, saying, “We must enter the kingdom of God through many persecutions.”
net.ACT_14_23: When they had appointed elders for them in the various churches, with prayer and fasting they entrusted them to the protection of the Lord in whom they had believed.
net.ACT_14_24: Then they passed through Pisidia and came into Pamphylia,
net.ACT_14_25: and when they had spoken the word in Perga, they went down to Attalia.
net.ACT_14_26: From there they sailed back to Antioch, where they had been commended to the grace of God for the work they had now completed.
net.ACT_14_27: When they arrived and gathered the church together, they reported all the things God had done with them, and that he had opened a door of faith for the Gentiles.
net.ACT_14_28: So they spent considerable time with the disciples.
net.ACT_15_1: Now some men came down from Judea and began to teach the brothers, “Unless you are circumcised according to the custom of Moses, you cannot be saved.”
net.ACT_15_2: When Paul and Barnabas had a major argument and debate with them, the church appointed Paul and Barnabas and some others from among them to go up to meet with the apostles and elders in Jerusalem about this point of disagreement.
net.ACT_15_3: So they were sent on their way by the church, and as they passed through both Phoenicia and Samaria, they were relating at length the conversion of the Gentiles and bringing great joy to all the brothers.
net.ACT_15_4: When they arrived in Jerusalem, they were received by the church and the apostles and the elders, and they reported all the things God had done with them.
net.ACT_15_5: But some from the religious party of the Pharisees who had believed stood up and said, “It is necessary to circumcise the Gentiles and to order them to observe the law of Moses.”
net.ACT_15_6: Both the apostles and the elders met together to deliberate about this matter.
net.ACT_15_7: After there had been much debate, Peter stood up and said to them, “Brothers, you know that some time ago God chose me to preach to the Gentiles so they would hear the message of the gospel and believe.
net.ACT_15_8: And God, who knows the heart, has testified to them by giving them the Holy Spirit just as he did to us,
net.ACT_15_9: and he made no distinction between them and us, cleansing their hearts by faith.
net.ACT_15_10: So now why are you putting God to the test by placing on the neck of the disciples a yoke that neither our ancestors nor we have been able to bear?
net.ACT_15_11: On the contrary, we believe that we are saved through the grace of the Lord Jesus, in the same way as they are.”
net.ACT_15_12: The whole group kept quiet and listened to Barnabas and Paul while they explained all the miraculous signs and wonders God had done among the Gentiles through them.
net.ACT_15_13: After they stopped speaking, James replied, “Brothers, listen to me.
net.ACT_15_14: Simeon has explained how God first concerned himself to select from among the Gentiles a people for his name.
net.ACT_15_15: The words of the prophets agree with this, as it is written,
net.ACT_15_16: ‘After this I will return, and I will rebuild the fallen tent of David; I will rebuild its ruins and restore it,
net.ACT_15_17: so that the rest of humanity may seek the Lord, namely, all the Gentiles I have called to be my own,’ says the Lord, who makes these things
net.ACT_15_19: “Therefore I conclude that we should not cause extra difficulty for those among the Gentiles who are turning to God,
net.ACT_15_20: but that we should write them a letter telling them to abstain from things defiled by idols and from sexual immorality and from what has been strangled and from blood.
net.ACT_15_21: For Moses has had those who proclaim him in every town from ancient times, because he is read aloud in the synagogues every Sabbath.”
net.ACT_15_22: Then the apostles and elders, with the whole church, decided to send men chosen from among them, Judas called Barsabbas and Silas, leaders among the brothers, to Antioch with Paul and Barnabas.
net.ACT_15_23: They sent this letter with them: From the apostles and elders, your brothers, to the Gentile brothers and sisters in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia, greetings!
net.ACT_15_24: Since we have heard that some have gone out from among us with no orders from us and have confused you, upsetting your minds by what they said,
net.ACT_15_25: we have unanimously decided to choose men to send to you along with our dear friends Barnabas and Paul,
net.ACT_15_26: who have risked their lives for the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
net.ACT_15_27: Therefore we are sending Judas and Silas who will tell you these things themselves in person.
net.ACT_15_28: For it seemed best to the Holy Spirit and to us not to place any greater burden on you than these necessary rules:
net.ACT_15_29: that you abstain from meat that has been sacrificed to idols and from blood and from what has been strangled and from sexual immorality. If you keep yourselves from doing these things, you will do well. Farewell.
net.ACT_15_30: So when they were dismissed, they went down to Antioch, and after gathering the entire group together, they delivered the letter.
net.ACT_15_31: When they read it aloud, the people rejoiced at its encouragement.
net.ACT_15_32: Both Judas and Silas, who were prophets themselves, encouraged and strengthened the brothers with a long speech.
net.ACT_15_33: After they had spent some time there, they were sent off in peace by the brothers to those who had sent them.
net.ACT_15_35: But Paul and Barnabas remained in Antioch, teaching and proclaiming (along with many others) the word of the Lord. Paul and Barnabas Part Company
net.ACT_15_36: After some days Paul said to Barnabas, “Let’s return and visit the brothers in every town where we proclaimed the word of the Lord to see how they are doing.”
net.ACT_15_37: Barnabas wanted to bring John called Mark along with them too,
net.ACT_15_38: but Paul insisted that they should not take along this one who had left them in Pamphylia and had not accompanied them in the work.
net.ACT_15_39: They had a sharp disagreement, so that they parted company. Barnabas took along Mark and sailed away to Cyprus,
net.ACT_15_40: but Paul chose Silas and set out, commended to the grace of the Lord by the brothers and sisters.
net.ACT_15_41: He passed through Syria and Cilicia, strengthening the churches.
net.ACT_16_1: He also came to Derbe and to Lystra. A disciple named Timothy was there, the son of a Jewish woman who was a believer, but whose father was a Greek.
net.ACT_16_2: The brothers in Lystra and Iconium spoke well of him.
net.ACT_16_3: Paul wanted Timothy to accompany him, and he took him and circumcised him because of the Jews who were in those places, for they all knew that his father was Greek.
net.ACT_16_4: As they went through the towns, they passed on the decrees that had been decided on by the apostles and elders in Jerusalem for the Gentile believers to obey.
net.ACT_16_5: So the churches were being strengthened in the faith and were increasing in number every day. Paul’s Vision of the Macedonian Man
net.ACT_16_6: They went through the region of Phrygia and Galatia, having been prevented by the Holy Spirit from speaking the message in the province of Asia.
net.ACT_16_7: When they came to Mysia, they attempted to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus did not allow them to do this,
net.ACT_16_8: so they passed through Mysia and went down to Troas.
net.ACT_16_9: A vision appeared to Paul during the night: A Macedonian man was standing there urging him, “Come over to Macedonia and help us!”
net.ACT_16_10: After Paul saw the vision, we attempted immediately to go over to Macedonia, concluding that God had called us to proclaim the good news to them. Arrival at Philippi
net.ACT_16_11: We put out to sea from Troas and sailed a straight course to Samothrace, the next day to Neapolis,
net.ACT_16_12: and from there to Philippi, which is a leading city of that district of Macedonia, a Roman colony. We stayed in this city for some days.
net.ACT_16_13: On the Sabbath day we went outside the city gate to the side of the river, where we thought there would be a place of prayer, and we sat down and began to speak to the women who had assembled there.
net.ACT_16_14: A woman named Lydia, a dealer in purple cloth from the city of Thyatira, a God-fearing woman, listened to us. The Lord opened her heart to respond to what Paul was saying.
net.ACT_16_15: After she and her household were baptized, she urged us, “If you consider me to be a believer in the Lord, come and stay in my house.” And she persuaded us. Paul and Silas Are Thrown Into Prison
net.ACT_16_16: Now as we were going to the place of prayer, a slave girl met us who had a spirit that enabled her to foretell the future by supernatural means. She brought her owners a great profit by fortune-telling.
net.ACT_16_17: She followed behind Paul and us and kept crying out, “These men are servants of the Most High God, who are proclaiming to you the way of salvation.”
net.ACT_16_18: She continued to do this for many days. But Paul became greatly annoyed, and turned and said to the spirit, “I command you in the name of Jesus Christ to come out of her!” And it came out of her at once.
net.ACT_16_19: But when her owners saw their hope of profit was gone, they seized Paul and Silas and dragged them into the marketplace before the authorities.
net.ACT_16_20: When they had brought them before the magistrates, they said, “These men are throwing our city into confusion. They are Jews
net.ACT_16_21: and are advocating customs that are not lawful for us to accept or practice, since we are Romans.”
net.ACT_16_22: The crowd joined the attack against them, and the magistrates tore the clothes off Paul and Silas and ordered them to be beaten with rods.
net.ACT_16_23: After they had beaten them severely, they threw them into prison and commanded the jailer to guard them securely.
net.ACT_16_24: Receiving such orders, he threw them in the inner cell and fastened their feet in the stocks.
net.ACT_16_25: About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns to God, and the rest of the prisoners were listening to them.
net.ACT_16_26: Suddenly a great earthquake occurred, so that the foundations of the prison were shaken. Immediately all the doors flew open, and the bonds of all the prisoners came loose.
net.ACT_16_27: When the jailer woke up and saw the doors of the prison standing open, he drew his sword and was about to kill himself, because he assumed the prisoners had escaped.
net.ACT_16_28: But Paul called out loudly, “Do not harm yourself, for we are all here!”
net.ACT_16_29: Calling for lights, the jailer rushed in and fell down trembling at the feet of Paul and Silas.
net.ACT_16_30: Then he brought them outside and asked, “Sirs, what must I do to be saved?”
net.ACT_16_31: They replied, “Believe in the Lord Jesus and you will be saved, you and your household.”
net.ACT_16_32: Then they spoke the word of the Lord to him, along with all those who were in his house.
net.ACT_16_33: At that hour of the night he took them and washed their wounds; then he and all his family were baptized right away.
net.ACT_16_34: The jailer brought them into his house and set food before them, and he rejoiced greatly that he had come to believe in God, together with his entire household.
net.ACT_16_35: At daybreak the magistrates sent their police officers, saying, “Release those men.”
net.ACT_16_36: The jailer reported these words to Paul, saying, “The magistrates have sent orders to release you. So come out now and go in peace.”
net.ACT_16_37: But Paul said to the police officers, “They had us beaten in public without a proper trial —even though we are Roman citizens —and they threw us in prison. And now they want to send us away secretly? Absolutely not! They themselves must come and escort us out!”
net.ACT_16_38: The police officers reported these words to the magistrates. They were frightened when they heard Paul and Silas were Roman citizens
net.ACT_16_39: and came and apologized to them. After they brought them out, they asked them repeatedly to leave the city.
net.ACT_16_40: When they came out of the prison, they entered Lydia’s house, and when they saw the brothers, they encouraged them and then departed.
net.ACT_17_1: After they traveled through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they came to Thessalonica, where there was a Jewish synagogue.
net.ACT_17_2: Paul went to the Jews in the synagogue, as he customarily did, and on three Sabbath days he addressed them from the scriptures,
net.ACT_17_3: explaining and demonstrating that the Christ had to suffer and to rise from the dead, saying, “This Jesus I am proclaiming to you is the Christ.”
net.ACT_17_4: Some of them were persuaded and joined Paul and Silas, along with a large group of God-fearing Greeks and quite a few prominent women.
net.ACT_17_5: But the Jews became jealous, and gathering together some worthless men from the rabble in the marketplace, they formed a mob and set the city in an uproar. They attacked Jason’s house, trying to find Paul and Silas to bring them out to the assembly.
net.ACT_17_6: When they did not find them, they dragged Jason and some of the brothers before the city officials, screaming, “These people who have stirred up trouble throughout the world have come here too,
net.ACT_17_7: and Jason has welcomed them as guests! They are all acting against Caesar’s decrees, saying there is another king named Jesus!”
net.ACT_17_8: They caused confusion among the crowd and the city officials who heard these things.
net.ACT_17_9: After the city officials had received bail from Jason and the others, they released them. Paul and Silas at Berea
net.ACT_17_10: The brothers sent Paul and Silas off to Berea at once, during the night. When they arrived, they went to the Jewish synagogue.
net.ACT_17_11: These Jews were more open-minded than those in Thessalonica, for they eagerly received the message, examining the scriptures carefully every day to see if these things were so.
net.ACT_17_12: Therefore many of them believed, along with quite a few prominent Greek women and men.
net.ACT_17_13: But when the Jews from Thessalonica heard that Paul had also proclaimed the word of God in Berea, they came there too, inciting and disturbing the crowds.
net.ACT_17_14: Then the brothers sent Paul away to the coast at once, but Silas and Timothy remained in Berea.
net.ACT_17_15: Those who accompanied Paul escorted him as far as Athens, and after receiving an order for Silas and Timothy to come to him as soon as possible, they left. Paul at Athens
net.ACT_17_16: While Paul was waiting for them in Athens, his spirit was greatly upset because he saw the city was full of idols.
net.ACT_17_17: So he was addressing the Jews and the God-fearing Gentiles in the synagogue, and in the marketplace every day those who happened to be there.
net.ACT_17_18: Also some of the Epicurean and Stoic philosophers were conversing with him, and some were asking, “What does this foolish babbler want to say?” Others said, “He seems to be a proclaimer of foreign gods.” (They said this because he was proclaiming the good news about Jesus and the resurrection.)
net.ACT_17_19: So they took Paul and brought him to the Areopagus, saying, “May we know what this new teaching is that you are proclaiming?
net.ACT_17_20: For you are bringing some surprising things to our ears, so we want to know what they mean.”
net.ACT_17_21: (All the Athenians and the foreigners who lived there used to spend their time in nothing else than telling or listening to something new.)
net.ACT_17_22: So Paul stood before the Areopagus and said, “Men of Athens, I see that you are very religious in all respects.
net.ACT_17_23: For as I went around and observed closely your objects of worship, I even found an altar with this inscription: ‘To an unknown god.’ Therefore what you worship without knowing it, this I proclaim to you.
net.ACT_17_24: The God who made the world and everything in it, who is Lord of heaven and earth, does not live in temples made by human hands,
net.ACT_17_25: nor is he served by human hands, as if he needed anything, because he himself gives life and breath and everything to everyone.
net.ACT_17_26: From one man he made every nation of the human race to inhabit the entire earth, determining their set times and the fixed limits of the places where they would live,
net.ACT_17_27: so that they would search for God and perhaps grope around for him and find him, though he is not far from each one of us.
net.ACT_17_28: For in him we live and move about and exist, as even some of your own poets have said, ‘For we too are his offspring.’
net.ACT_17_29: So since we are God’s offspring, we should not think the deity is like gold or silver or stone, an image made by human skill and imagination.
net.ACT_17_30: Therefore, although God has overlooked such times of ignorance, he now commands all people everywhere to repent,
net.ACT_17_31: because he has set a day on which he is going to judge the world in righteousness, by a man whom he designated, having provided proof to everyone by raising him from the dead.”
net.ACT_17_32: Now when they heard about the resurrection from the dead, some began to scoff, but others said, “We will hear you again about this.”
net.ACT_17_34: But some people joined him and believed. Among them were Dionysius, who was a member of the Areopagus, a woman named Damaris, and others with them.
net.ACT_18_1: After this Paul departed from Athens and went to Corinth.
net.ACT_18_2: There he found a Jew named Aquila, a native of Pontus, who had recently come from Italy with his wife Priscilla, because Claudius had ordered all the Jews to depart from Rome. Paul approached them,
net.ACT_18_3: and because he worked at the same trade, he stayed with them and worked with them (for they were tentmakers by trade).
net.ACT_18_4: He addressed both Jews and Greeks in the synagogue every Sabbath, attempting to persuade them.
net.ACT_18_5: Now when Silas and Timothy arrived from Macedonia, Paul became wholly absorbed with proclaiming the word, testifying to the Jews that Jesus was the Christ.
net.ACT_18_6: When they opposed him and reviled him, he protested by shaking out his clothes and said to them, “Your blood be on your own heads! I am guiltless! From now on I will go to the Gentiles!”
net.ACT_18_7: Then Paul left the synagogue and went to the house of a person named Titius Justus, a Gentile who worshiped God, whose house was next door to the synagogue.
net.ACT_18_8: Crispus, the president of the synagogue, believed in the Lord together with his entire household, and many of the Corinthians who heard about it believed and were baptized.
net.ACT_18_9: The Lord said to Paul by a vision in the night, “Do not be afraid, but speak and do not be silent,
net.ACT_18_10: because I am with you, and no one will assault you to harm you, because I have many people in this city.”
net.ACT_18_11: So he stayed there a year and six months, teaching the word of God among them. Paul Before the Proconsul Gallio
net.ACT_18_12: Now while Gallio was proconsul of Achaia, the Jews attacked Paul together and brought him before the judgment seat,
net.ACT_18_13: saying, “This man is persuading people to worship God in a way contrary to the law!”
net.ACT_18_14: But just as Paul was about to speak, Gallio said to the Jews, “If it were a matter of some crime or serious piece of villainy, I would have been justified in accepting the complaint of you Jews,
net.ACT_18_15: but since it concerns points of disagreement about words and names and your own law, settle it yourselves. I will not be a judge of these things!”
net.ACT_18_16: Then he had them forced away from the judgment seat.
net.ACT_18_17: So they all seized Sosthenes, the president of the synagogue, and began to beat him in front of the judgment seat. Yet none of these things were of any concern to Gallio. Paul Returns to Antioch in Syria
net.ACT_18_18: Paul, after staying many more days in Corinth, said farewell to the brothers and sailed away to Syria accompanied by Priscilla and Aquila. He had his hair cut off at Cenchrea because he had made a vow.
net.ACT_18_19: When they reached Ephesus, Paul left Priscilla and Aquila behind there, but he himself went into the synagogue and addressed the Jews.
net.ACT_18_20: When they asked him to stay longer, he would not consent,
net.ACT_18_21: but said farewell to them and added, “I will come back to you again if God wills.” Then he set sail from Ephesus,
net.ACT_18_22: and when he arrived at Caesarea, he went up and greeted the church at Jerusalem and then went down to Antioch.
net.ACT_18_23: After he spent some time there, Paul left and went through the region of Galatia and Phrygia, strengthening all the disciples. Apollos Begins His Ministry
net.ACT_18_24: Now a Jew named Apollos, a native of Alexandria, arrived in Ephesus. He was an eloquent speaker, well-versed in the scriptures.
net.ACT_18_25: He had been instructed in the way of the Lord, and with great enthusiasm he spoke and taught accurately the facts about Jesus, although he knew only the baptism of John.
net.ACT_18_26: He began to speak out fearlessly in the synagogue, but when Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they took him aside and explained the way of God to him more accurately.
net.ACT_18_27: When Apollos wanted to cross over to Achaia, the brothers encouraged him and wrote to the disciples to welcome him. When he arrived, he assisted greatly those who had believed by grace,
net.ACT_18_28: for he refuted the Jews vigorously in public debate, demonstrating from the scriptures that the Christ was Jesus.
net.ACT_19_1: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul went through the inland regions and came to Ephesus. He found some disciples there
net.ACT_19_2: and said to them, “Did you receive the Holy Spirit when you believed?” They replied, “No, we have not even heard that there is a Holy Spirit.”
net.ACT_19_3: So Paul said, “Into what then were you baptized?” “Into John’s baptism,” they replied.
net.ACT_19_4: Paul said, “John baptized with a baptism of repentance, telling the people to believe in the one who was to come after him, that is, in Jesus.”
net.ACT_19_5: When they heard this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus,
net.ACT_19_6: and when Paul placed his hands on them, the Holy Spirit came upon them, and they began to speak in tongues and to prophesy.
net.ACT_19_7: (Now there were about twelve men in all.) Paul Continues to Minister at Ephesus
net.ACT_19_8: So Paul entered the synagogue and spoke out fearlessly for three months, addressing and convincing them about the kingdom of God.
net.ACT_19_9: But when some were stubborn and refused to believe, reviling the Way before the congregation, he left them and took the disciples with him, addressing them every day in the lecture hall of Tyrannus.
net.ACT_19_10: This went on for two years, so that all who lived in the province of Asia, both Jews and Greeks, heard the word of the Lord. The Seven Sons of Sceva
net.ACT_19_11: God was performing extraordinary miracles by Paul’s hands,
net.ACT_19_12: so that when even handkerchiefs or aprons that had touched his body were brought to the sick, their diseases left them and the evil spirits went out of them.
net.ACT_19_13: But some itinerant Jewish exorcists tried to invoke the name of the Lord Jesus over those who were possessed by evil spirits, saying, “I sternly warn you by Jesus whom Paul preaches.”
net.ACT_19_14: (Now seven sons of a man named Sceva, a Jewish high priest, were doing this.)
net.ACT_19_15: But the evil spirit replied to them, “I know about Jesus and I am acquainted with Paul, but who are you?”
net.ACT_19_16: Then the man who was possessed by the evil spirit jumped on them and beat them all into submission. He prevailed against them so that they fled from that house naked and wounded.
net.ACT_19_17: This became known to all who lived in Ephesus, both Jews and Greeks; fear came over them all, and the name of the Lord Jesus was praised.
net.ACT_19_18: Many of those who had believed came forward, confessing and making their deeds known.
net.ACT_19_19: Large numbers of those who had practiced magic collected their books and burned them up in the presence of everyone. When the value of the books was added up, it was found to total 50,000 silver coins.
net.ACT_19_20: In this way the word of the Lord continued to grow in power and to prevail. A Riot in Ephesus
net.ACT_19_21: Now after all these things had taken place, Paul resolved to go to Jerusalem, passing through Macedonia and Achaia. He said, “After I have been there, I must also see Rome.”
net.ACT_19_22: So after sending two of his assistants, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia, he himself stayed on for a while in the province of Asia.
net.ACT_19_23: At that time a great disturbance took place concerning the Way.
net.ACT_19_24: For a man named Demetrius, a silversmith who made silver shrines of Artemis, brought a great deal of business to the craftsmen.
net.ACT_19_25: He gathered these together, along with the workmen in similar trades, and said, “Men, you know that our prosperity comes from this business.
net.ACT_19_26: And you see and hear that this Paul has persuaded and turned away a large crowd, not only in Ephesus but in practically all of the province of Asia, by saying that gods made by hands are not gods at all.
net.ACT_19_27: There is danger not only that this business of ours will come into disrepute, but also that the temple of the great goddess Artemis will be regarded as nothing, and she whom all the province of Asia and the world worship will suffer the loss of her greatness.”
net.ACT_19_28: When they heard this they became enraged and began to shout, “Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!”
net.ACT_19_29: The city was filled with the uproar, and the crowd rushed to the theater together, dragging with them Gaius and Aristarchus, the Macedonians who were Paul’s traveling companions.
net.ACT_19_30: But when Paul wanted to enter the public assembly, the disciples would not let him.
net.ACT_19_31: Even some of the provincial authorities who were his friends sent a message to him, urging him not to venture into the theater.
net.ACT_19_32: So then some were shouting one thing, some another, for the assembly was in confusion, and most of them did not know why they had met together.
net.ACT_19_33: Some of the crowd concluded it was about Alexander because the Jews had pushed him to the front. Alexander, gesturing with his hand, was wanting to make a defense before the public assembly.
net.ACT_19_34: But when they recognized that he was a Jew, they all shouted in unison, “Great is Artemis of the Ephesians!” for about two hours.
net.ACT_19_35: After the city secretary quieted the crowd, he said, “Men of Ephesus, what person is there who does not know that the city of the Ephesians is the keeper of the temple of the great Artemis and of her image that fell from heaven?
net.ACT_19_36: So because these facts are indisputable, you must keep quiet and not do anything reckless.
net.ACT_19_37: For you have brought these men here who are neither temple robbers nor blasphemers of our goddess.
net.ACT_19_38: If then Demetrius and the craftsmen who are with him have a complaint against someone, the courts are open and there are proconsuls; let them bring charges against one another there.
net.ACT_19_39: But if you want anything in addition, it will have to be settled in a legal assembly.
net.ACT_19_40: For we are in danger of being charged with rioting today, since there is no cause we can give to explain this disorderly gathering.”
net.ACT_19_41: After he had said this, he dismissed the assembly.
net.ACT_20_1: After the disturbance had ended, Paul sent for the disciples, and after encouraging them and saying farewell, he left to go to Macedonia.
net.ACT_20_2: After he had gone through those regions and spoken many words of encouragement to the believers there, he came to Greece,
net.ACT_20_3: where he stayed for three months. Because the Jews had made a plot against him as he was intending to sail for Syria, he decided to return through Macedonia.
net.ACT_20_4: Paul was accompanied by Sopater son of Pyrrhus from Berea, Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica, Gaius from Derbe, and Timothy, as well as Tychicus and Trophimus from the province of Asia.
net.ACT_20_5: These had gone on ahead and were waiting for us in Troas.
net.ACT_20_6: We sailed away from Philippi after the days of Unleavened Bread, and within five days we came to the others in Troas, where we stayed for seven days.
net.ACT_20_7: On the first day of the week, when we met to break bread, Paul began to speak to the people, and because he intended to leave the next day, he extended his message until midnight.
net.ACT_20_8: (Now there were many lamps in the upstairs room where we were meeting.)
net.ACT_20_9: A young man named Eutychus, who was sitting in the window, was sinking into a deep sleep while Paul continued to speak for a long time. Fast asleep, he fell down from the third story and was picked up dead.
net.ACT_20_10: But Paul went down, threw himself on the young man, put his arms around him, and said, “Do not be distressed, for he is still alive!”
net.ACT_20_11: Then Paul went back upstairs, and after he had broken bread and eaten, he talked with them a long time, until dawn. Then he left.
net.ACT_20_12: They took the boy home alive and were greatly comforted. The Voyage to Miletus
net.ACT_20_13: We went on ahead to the ship and put out to sea for Assos, intending to take Paul aboard there, for he had arranged it this way. He himself was intending to go there by land.
net.ACT_20_14: When he met us in Assos, we took him aboard and went to Mitylene.
net.ACT_20_15: We set sail from there, and on the following day we arrived off Chios. The next day we approached Samos, and the day after that we arrived at Miletus.
net.ACT_20_16: For Paul had decided to sail past Ephesus so as not to spend time in the province of Asia, for he was hurrying to arrive in Jerusalem, if possible, by the day of Pentecost.
net.ACT_20_17: From Miletus he sent a message to Ephesus, telling the elders of the church to come to him.
net.ACT_20_18: When they arrived, he said to them, “You yourselves know how I lived the whole time I was with you, from the first day I set foot in the province of Asia,
net.ACT_20_19: serving the Lord with all humility and with tears, and with the trials that happened to me because of the plots of the Jews.
net.ACT_20_20: You know that I did not hold back from proclaiming to you anything that would be helpful, and from teaching you publicly and from house to house,
net.ACT_20_21: testifying to both Jews and Greeks about repentance toward God and faith in our Lord Jesus.
net.ACT_20_22: And now, compelled by the Spirit, I am going to Jerusalem without knowing what will happen to me there,
net.ACT_20_23: except that the Holy Spirit warns me in town after town that imprisonment and persecutions are waiting for me.
net.ACT_20_24: But I do not consider my life worth anything to myself, so that I may finish my task and the ministry that I received from the Lord Jesus, to testify to the good news of God’s grace.
net.ACT_20_25: “And now I know that none of you among whom I went around proclaiming the kingdom will see me again.
net.ACT_20_26: Therefore I declare to you today that I am innocent of the blood of you all.
net.ACT_20_27: For I did not hold back from announcing to you the whole purpose of God.
net.ACT_20_28: Watch out for yourselves and for all the flock of which the Holy Spirit has made you overseers, to shepherd the church of God that he obtained with the blood of his own Son.
net.ACT_20_29: I know that after I am gone fierce wolves will come in among you, not sparing the flock.
net.ACT_20_30: Even from among your own group men will arise, teaching perversions of the truth to draw the disciples away after them.
net.ACT_20_31: Therefore be alert, remembering that night and day for three years I did not stop warning each one of you with tears.
net.ACT_20_32: And now I entrust you to God and to the message of his grace. This message is able to build you up and give you an inheritance among all those who are sanctified.
net.ACT_20_33: I have desired no one’s silver or gold or clothing.
net.ACT_20_34: You yourselves know that these hands of mine provided for my needs and the needs of those who were with me.
net.ACT_20_35: By all these things, I have shown you that by working in this way we must help the weak, and remember the words of the Lord Jesus that he himself said, ‘It is more blessed to give than to receive.’”
net.ACT_20_36: When he had said these things, he knelt down with them all and prayed.
net.ACT_20_37: They all began to weep loudly, and hugged Paul and kissed him,
net.ACT_20_38: especially saddened by what he had said, that they were not going to see him again. Then they accompanied him to the ship.
net.ACT_21_1: After we tore ourselves away from them, we put out to sea, and sailing a straight course, we came to Cos, on the next day to Rhodes, and from there to Patara.
net.ACT_21_2: We found a ship crossing over to Phoenicia, went aboard, and put out to sea.
net.ACT_21_3: After we sighted Cyprus and left it behind on our port side, we sailed on to Syria and put in at Tyre, because the ship was to unload its cargo there.
net.ACT_21_4: After we located the disciples, we stayed there seven days. They repeatedly told Paul through the Spirit not to set foot in Jerusalem.
net.ACT_21_5: When our time was over, we left and went on our way. All of them, with their wives and children, accompanied us outside of the city. After kneeling down on the beach and praying,
net.ACT_21_6: we said farewell to one another. Then we went aboard the ship, and they returned to their own homes.
net.ACT_21_7: We continued the voyage from Tyre and arrived at Ptolemais, and when we had greeted the brothers, we stayed with them for one day.
net.ACT_21_8: On the next day we left and came to Caesarea, and entered the house of Philip the evangelist, who was one of the seven, and stayed with him.
net.ACT_21_9: (He had four unmarried daughters who prophesied.)
net.ACT_21_10: While we remained there for a number of days, a prophet named Agabus came down from Judea.
net.ACT_21_11: He came to us, took Paul’s belt, tied his own hands and feet with it, and said, “The Holy Spirit says this: ‘This is the way the Jews in Jerusalem will tie up the man whose belt this is, and will hand him over to the Gentiles.’”
net.ACT_21_12: When we heard this, both we and the local people begged him not to go up to Jerusalem.
net.ACT_21_13: Then Paul replied, “What are you doing, weeping and breaking my heart? For I am ready not only to be tied up, but even to die in Jerusalem for the name of the Lord Jesus.”
net.ACT_21_14: Because he could not be persuaded, we said no more except, “The Lord’s will be done.”
net.ACT_21_15: After these days we got ready and started up to Jerusalem.
net.ACT_21_16: Some of the disciples from Caesarea came along with us too, and brought us to the house of Mnason of Cyprus, a disciple from the earliest times, with whom we were to stay.
net.ACT_21_17: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the brothers welcomed us gladly.
net.ACT_21_18: The next day Paul went in with us to see James, and all the elders were there.
net.ACT_21_19: When Paul had greeted them, he began to explain in detail what God had done among the Gentiles through his ministry.
net.ACT_21_20: When they heard this, they praised God. Then they said to him, “You see, brother, how many thousands of Jews there are who have believed, and they are all ardent observers of the law.
net.ACT_21_21: They have been informed about you—that you teach all the Jews now living among the Gentiles to abandon Moses, telling them not to circumcise their children or live according to our customs.
net.ACT_21_22: What then should we do? They will no doubt hear that you have come.
net.ACT_21_23: So do what we tell you: We have four men who have taken a vow;
